tagline: An affinity-matrix where psychic power distribution, spiritual resonance, and emotional pressure determine outcomes more than raw esper ability
visualizationReason: Mob Psycho 100 is a psychic power network organized as an affinity-matrix. The affinity-matrix captures the core structural thesis: each character has a psychic power level and an emotional intelligence level, and the relationship between these two dimensions determines the character's arc. Espers with high power and low emotional intelligence become antagonists; those with balanced development become allies.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: I'll become a doctor, fall in love, become a full-fledged member of society. One percent at a time.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYSTEM STATUS]: PSYCHIC EMISSION DETECTED — EMOTIONAL PRESSURE: 0%

introductionSummary: Mob Psycho 100 operates as an affinity-matrix where psychic power is distributed across individuals with radically different relationships to the spiritual world. The analytical lens is psychological: power level doesn't determine outcome — emotional state, acceptance, and genuine human connection determine it. The series' thesis is that overwhelming psychic power is meaningless without the emotional intelligence to use it responsibly. The core mechanic is emotional pressure — Mob suppresses his power to avoid hurting others, and when that pressure exceeds his threshold, catastrophic psychic events occur.

hero.microHook: Psychic power is the x-axis; emotional intelligence is the y-axis.
hero.thesis: An affinity-matrix where raw psychic power is irrelevant compared to emotional intelligence and genuine human connection.

aiInsights.casual: Mob Psycho 100 is about a kid who could destroy the world with his mind but just wants to be normal. He's an extremely polite middle schooler who works part-time for a con-artist psychic, joined the Body Improvement Club to try to build muscle, and is completely oblivious to the girl who has a crush on him. It's the most wholesome anime about a walking apocalypse you'll ever see. The 'villains' are mostly people with hurt feelings who need a hug.
aiInsights.deep: Mob Psycho 100's system is a psychological power matrix where raw psychic ability and emotional development exist on separate axes. The series' structural insight is that these two dimensions are independent — you can have overwhelming power with zero emotional development (the Claw espers), moderate power with high emotional development (Reigen), or the perfect balance (Mob at the end). The affinity-matrix is not about who has the most power; it's about who has developed the emotional framework to handle what they have. Mob's genius is that he doesn't want power — he wants ordinary human connection. This makes him the only esper in the series who is genuinely free from the corruption of power because he never wanted it in the first place. The con-artist Reigen is actually the most emotionally intelligent character — he understands people better than anyone, which is why he can 'sense' things without psychic powers.

powerSystem.name: Psychic Powers (Esper)
powerSystem.subtitle: Telekinesis and Spiritual Resonance
powerSystem.loreDesc: The primary power system — telekinesis, telepathy, psychokinesis, and spirit manipulation. Psychic power ranges from moving small objects to reality-warping levels. Power level is fixed per individual but emotional state affects output.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The fixed-potential system — psychic power is an innate capacity that cannot be increased through training (unlike ki or chakra). This makes espers fundamentally unequal from birth. The only variable is emotional state affecting output efficiency.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mob at 100% emotional release destroying the Claw psychic army in seconds — the terrifying gap between his suppressed state and full power.

powerSystem.name: Emotional Suppression
powerSystem.subtitle: The 0% Pressure Technique
powerSystem.loreDesc: Mob's unique technique of suppressing psychic output by suppressing emotional expression. He maintains emotional pressure at 0% by never getting angry, never showing off, never being greedy. This prevents accidents but also prevents genuine self-expression.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The emotional dampening mechanic — Mob's technique of emotional suppression is a form of psychic output control. When emotional pressure builds (typically from feelings of inadequacy or injustice), it compresses into a psychic explosion at threshold. The technique works but creates an identity crisis — who is Mob when he never expresses his true feelings?
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mob maintaining 0% emotional pressure while being bullied — the incredible discipline of someone who refuses to let their power hurt others.

powerSystem.name: Spiritual Affinity
powerSystem.subtitle: Esper-Spirit Connection
powerSystem.loreDesc: The connection between espers and spirits. High spiritual affinity means stronger psychic resonance and more powerful spirit interactions. Mob has extremely high spiritual affinity — he can perceive and interact with spirits naturally.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The spiritual perception layer — some characters can perceive spirits, others cannot. This creates a perception asymmetry where non-psychics live in a fundamentally different world than espers. Ekubo's existence as a powerful spirit depends on finding espers with sufficient affinity.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mob perceiving spirits clearly while others see nothing — the first indication that his power includes spiritual perception.

powerSystem.name: Body Improvement
powerSystem.subtitle: The Physical Esper Alternative
powerSystem.loreDesc: The Body Improvement Club's philosophy: physical training can achieve comparable results to psychic powers through discipline. They represent the human potential counter to supernatural gifts — muscle as an alternative to telekinesis.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The non-esper path — Body Improvement demonstrates that human physical potential can approach esper levels through sufficient training. This creates a philosophical counter to the esper hierarchy: maybe psychics aren't superior.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The Body Improvement Club standing up to espers — proof that ordinary humans can face supernatural threats through courage and training.

powerSystem.name: 100% Emotional Release
powerSystem.subtitle: The Nuclear Option
powerSystem.loreDesc: When Mob's suppressed emotional pressure exceeds his threshold (typically from deep emotional hurt), he releases 100% of his psychic power. At full release, Mob is effectively a walking apocalypse — reality warps around him, matter disintegrates, and only emotional intervention can stop him.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The suppression-release cycle — Mob's power is a controlled nuclear reaction. He maintains low output through constant emotional suppression, but the accumulated pressure eventually releases catastrophically. The 100% state is not a choice but a system failure from accumulated emotional overload.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Mob's 100% release against Koyama — the moment the Body Improvement Club's friendship became more powerful than his psychic apocalypse.

rules.name: Psychic power is innate and fixed
rules.subtitle: The Fixed Potential Rule
rules.loreConsequence: Espers are born with their power ceiling. Training can improve technique but not raw output. This makes espers fundamentally unequal from birth — some people simply have more spiritual potential than others.
rules.systemEquivalent: An inborn inequality mechanism — psychic power cannot be increased, only expressed. This means the esper hierarchy is a genetic aristocracy, with no possibility of meritocratic advancement.

rules.name: Emotional state affects psychic output
rules.subtitle: The Emotional Pressure Gate
rules.loreConsequence: Mob suppresses his output by suppressing his emotions. Strong emotions (especially negative ones) dramatically increase psychic output — the series' version of Super Saiyan transformation.
rules.systemEquivalent: An emotional amplification gate — the only way to increase psychic output beyond baseline is through emotional amplification. This creates a psychological dependency on emotional state for combat effectiveness.

rules.name: Power without emotional intelligence creates villains
rules.subtitle: The Corruption Theorem
rules.loreConsequence: Every antagonist in the series is an esper with high power but low emotional development. The Claw President, Mogami, and every major villain represents what happens when someone gets power without the character to handle it.
rules.systemEquivalent: A character-development gate — raw power is meaningless without the emotional framework to contextualize it. The series' core argument: emotional intelligence is the prerequisite for responsible power use.

rules.name: Reigen has no psychic powers but senses everything
rules.subtitle: The Human Perception Theorem
rules.loreConsequence: Reigen's defining characteristic is that he has zero psychic ability but understands people better than any esper. He senses emotional truth through observation rather than telepathy.
rules.systemEquivalent: A human-potential proof — non-psychics can match espers in practical terms through superior emotional intelligence. Reigen's effectiveness proves that human social perception is a valid alternative to supernatural perception.

rules.name: Friendship and human connection suppress psychic power
rules.subtitle: The Connection Paradox
rules.loreConsequence: Mob's most powerful attacks are suppressed when he's reminded of his connections to people. The Body Improvement Club's friendship literally prevents him from going 100%.
rules.systemEquivalent: An emotional anchor mechanism — genuine human connection creates emotional stability that dampens psychic output. This is the system's built-in counter to power暴走.

characters.name: Mob (Shigeo Kageyama)
characters.title: esper with 100% Psychic Potential
characters.loreBio: A middle schooler with the highest psychic potential in the series. He suppresses 99% of his power to live normally — going to school, working part-time, joining the Body Improvement Club. His defining trait is his extraordinary emotional restraint and genuine kindness.
characters.systemBio: The protagonist as power ceiling — Mob represents the highest possible psychic power combined with the lowest possible desire to use it. His 100% emotional release is a system failure, not a choice. His arc is learning that emotional expression is not the same as emotional destruction.
characters.signatureMoment: Choosing to suppress his 100% power when the Body Improvement Club showed him genuine friendship — proving that connection is stronger than apocalypse.

characters.name: Reigen Arataka
characters.title: Psychic Consultant
characters.loreBio: A con-artist who pretends to be a psychic but actually has zero powers. Reigen is Mob's part-time boss and mentor. He understands human psychology better than any esper — his 'psychic sensing' is actually brilliant emotional reading.
characters.systemBio: The human intelligence node — Reigen's structural position is proof that non-psychics can match espers through superior emotional intelligence. He has zero psychic power but accomplishes more than any esper through understanding people.
characters.signatureMoment: Confronting the Claw President using only words and psychological insight — proving that human intelligence can defeat psychic power when the psychic has emotional vulnerabilities.

characters.name: Ekubo (Dimple)
characters.title: Powerful Spirit
characters.loreBio: A powerful spirit who initially wanted to use Mob to become a god. Ekubo represents the reformed villain arc — over the series he becomes genuinely invested in Mob's wellbeing and even sacrifices himself to protect him.
characters.systemBio: The spirit node — Ekubo's structural position is the series' proof that even initially selfish actors can develop genuine loyalty. His power as a spirit is independent of the esper system, making him one of the few non-esper entities with comparable capability.
characters.signatureMoment: Becoming a 'god' through the cult and then choosing to fight against his own followers to protect Mob — the reformed villain's redemption arc.

characters.name: Ritsu Kageyama
characters.title: Mob's Younger Brother
characters.loreBio: Mob's younger brother with latent psychic powers. Ritsu resents being overshadowed by his brother's overwhelming ability and struggles with feelings of inadequacy. When his powers awaken, they are initially oriented toward harmful thoughts.
characters.systemBio: The sibling rivalry node — Ritsu's structural position represents the pressure of being the less-powered sibling. His arc is learning that his worth isn't determined by psychic output.
characters.signatureMoment: Learning that his worth isn't determined by psychic output — Ritsu's acceptance of himself without powers is his character arc resolution.

characters.name: Teruki Hanazawa
characters.title: The Golden-Topped esper
characters.loreBio: The most naturally talented psychic in the series — handsome, popular, and powerful. Teruki starts as the series' most complete esper before his fight with Mob teaches him that power without understanding is hollow.
characters.systemBio: The talent node — Teruki's structural position is what happens when you give someone overwhelming power AND emotional intelligence without the character development to go with it. His arc is realizing that natural talent means nothing without genuine human connection.
characters.signatureMoment: Being defeated by Mob and learning that his 'confidence' was just arrogance — Teruki's defeat is the beginning of his real character development.

characters.name: Koyama
characters.title: Body Improvement Club President
characters.loreBio: The president of the Body Improvement Club — a group of physically trained students who serve as Mob's friends and support network. Koyama represents the human potential counter to psychic power.
characters.systemBio: The physical node — Koyama's structural position is proof that ordinary humans can face esper-level threats through physical training and courage. The Body Improvement Club is the system's argument that non-psychics are not automatically inferior.
characters.signatureMoment: Standing up to an esper psycically attacking Mob and saying he's stronger — the moment ordinary human courage matched psychic power.

characters.name: The Claw President (Suzuki)
characters.title: Claw Organization Leader
characters.loreBio: The leader of Claw, an organization of powerful espers who believe psychics should rule non-psychics. The Claw President has the second-highest psychic power after Mob and genuine megalomaniacal ambition.
characters.systemBio: The corruption node — Claw President's structural position is the clearest example of the corruption theorem: overwhelming power + zero emotional development = megalomania. He genuinely believes psychics are superior and non-psychics are insects.
characters.signatureMoment: Fighting Mob and revealing his 'superior esper' philosophy — the clearest articulation of what esper supremacy looks like in practice.

characters.name: Tenga Kawajiri
characters.title: Cult Member
characters.loreBio: A member of Ekubo's cult who represents the reformed bully arc. Tenga was a delinquent who joined the cult because he felt lost, and he eventually becomes one of Mob's genuine supporters.
characters.systemBio: The reformed antagonist node — Tenga's structural position represents the possibility of redemption through genuine connection. His journey from cult member to genuine friend shows that people can change.
characters.signatureMoment: Choosing to help Mob instead of Ekubo when the cult turned violent — the moment Tenga chose genuine connection over power.

characters.name: Sho Suzuki (Telepathy Arc)
characters.title: Telepathic esper
characters.loreBio: A powerful telepathic esper from the telepathy arc. Sho's powers emerged from emotional trauma and he uses them to inflict the same pain on others that he experienced.
characters.systemBio: The trauma node — Sho's structural position represents power emerging from emotional damage. His telepathy is a manifestation of unprocessed trauma rather than natural ability.
characters.signatureMoment: His telepathic assault on Mob's consciousness — revealing the emotional damage that created his power.

characters.name: Emi (Mob's Girlfriend)
characters.title: Mob's Love Interest
characters.loreBio: A girl from Mob's school who has a crush on him. Emi represents Mob's desire for ordinary human connection — a girlfriend, normal relationships, a normal life. Her presence is the counterweight to his overwhelming power.
characters.systemBio: The anchor node — Emi's structural position is Mob's emotional tether to normalcy. She is proof that Mob's deepest desire is not power but human connection.
characters.signatureMoment: Her genuine crush on Mob — showing that even someone who doesn't know about his powers is drawn to his authentic self.

factions.name: esper Network
factions.description: Individuals with psychic powers scattered across society. Espers range from powerful (Mob, Teruki) to street-level (Reigen's clients). They exist outside normal social hierarchies because their power gives them an alternative status system.
factions.loreDesc: The esper community is not formally organized except in crisis. Espers are distributed across society with no institutional structure — their hierarchy is purely based on power level.
factions.systemDesc: An informal power aristocracy — esper status is determined by psychic output, creating a natural hierarchy independent of social institutions. This makes espers inherently difficult to organize or govern.

factions.name: Body Improvement Club
factions.description: Mob's genuine friend group. A club of physically training students who provide Mob with human connection outside the esper world. They represent the power of ordinary human community.
factions.loreDesc: The Body Improvement Club is the most wholesome faction in anime — a group of friends who train physically and support each other. Their defining trait is that they have no powers but face esper threats anyway.
factions.systemDesc: The human community node — the Body Improvement Club is the system's proof that human connection is a valid alternative to psychic power. They are the emotional anchor that keeps Mob from going 100%.

factions.name: Claw (Company)
factions.description: A criminal organization of powerful espers led by the Claw President. Claw believes espers are superior to non-psychics and should rule society. They represent esper supremacy as a political ideology.
factions.loreDesc: Claw is an esper supremacist organization that recruits powerful espers and uses them to achieve political goals. Their ideology is that psychics are the next step in human evolution and non-psychics are inferior.
factions.systemDesc: A power-hierarchy ideology — Claw represents the logical endpoint of a society organized purely by psychic output: a genetic aristocracy of espers ruling over non-psychics as inferior beings.

factions.name: Spirits and Ghosts
factions.description: Non-human entities that exist in the spiritual world. Range from harmless to dangerous. Ekubo is the most powerful, but there are many others.
factions.loreDesc: Spirits in Mob Psycho 100 are separate from espers — they are non-human consciousnesses that exist independently of psychic powers. Most are minor threats; Ekubo is the apex.
factions.systemDesc: The parallel entity system — spirits represent an entirely separate category of supernatural being that exists alongside espers. They don't follow esper rules and have their own power hierarchy.

factions.name: Ekubo's Cult
factions.description: A group of followers Ekubo gathered believing he was a god. The cult represents how easily people are manipulated by the appearance of supernatural power.
factions.loreDesc: Ekubo's cult is a satirical look at how religious movements form around perceived supernatural authority. The members are ordinary people seeking meaning who found it in a powerful spirit's promises.
factions.systemDesc: The manipulation network — the cult represents the system's dark side: spirits can manipulate humans through perceived authority, and humans are vulnerable to that manipulation.

relationships.description: Reigen is Mob's mentor in life skills and emotional intelligence — the person who teaches him more about being human than any esper
Ekubo starts as an antagonist who wants to use Mob, but genuine connection transforms him into Mob's ally
Mob's protective feelings toward his brother — Ritsu's resentment of Mob's power creates tension that Mob desperately wants to resolve
Teruki starts as an arrogant powerful esper who must learn from Mob's example — the rival who forces Mob's growth through opposition
The Body Improvement Club is Mob's genuine friend group — the human community that prevents his psychic power from consuming him
Reigen has no powers but his psychological insight works against spirits and espers alike — Ekubo fears Reigen's ability to see through manipulation
The esper supremacist vs. the esper who doesn't want power — their conflict is ideological as much as physical
Tenga followed Ekubo into the cult but chose Mob when it mattered — the reformed follower who became genuine
Mob's crush represents his desire for normal human connection — the girlfriend he wants to have before the esper identity consumes him
Telepathy vs. raw psychic power — Sho's emotional attack forces Mob to confront his own suppressed feelings

rankings.description: Affinity-matrix ranking based on psychic power level and emotional development axis

tiers.name: Apocalypse Tier
tiers.description: Reality-warping potential; only limited by emotional suppression

tiers.name: Apex esper Tier
tiers.description: Can defeat entire military units; the elite of the esper world

tiers.name: Elite esper Tier
tiers.description: Powerful espers or spirits who can challenge apex tier

tiers.name: Mid esper Tier
tiers.description: Street-level espers with specific powerful abilities

tiers.name: Street esper Tier
tiers.description: Minor psychics with limited but useful abilities

tiers.name: Human Tier
tiers.description: No psychic powers but high emotional intelligence — effective against espers through other means

systemQuestions.question: Why does Mob suppress his powers?
systemQuestions.answer: Mob suppresses his powers to avoid hurting people he cares about. His 100% emotional release is involuntary — triggered by accumulated emotional pressure, not choice. The suppression is his attempt to live normally despite being a walking apocalypse.

systemQuestions.question: Why does Reigen have no psychic powers but seem to sense everything?
systemQuestions.answer: Reigen has zero psychic ability — his 'psychic sensing' is actually extremely accurate emotional and psychological observation. He understands people at a level that appears supernatural because most people don't actually listen to each other.

systemQuestions.question: What makes the Body Improvement Club effective against espers?
systemQuestions.answer: The Body Improvement Club's effectiveness is not physical — they can't actually fight espers. Their effectiveness is emotional: they provide Mob with genuine human connection that prevents his emotional pressure from building to the 100% release threshold.

systemQuestions.question: Why does every esper antagonist have emotional damage?
systemQuestions.answer: The series' core argument: power without emotional development creates villains. The Claw President, Sho Suzuki, Mogami — every esper who goes bad has unprocessed emotional trauma. The system is saying that psychic power attracts people with unresolved psychological wounds.

causalEvents.name: Mob's Parents Discover His Powers
causalEvents.trigger: Mob's psychic powers are discovered by his parents at a young age
causalEvents.consequence: Mob begins suppressing his powers and emotions to avoid frightening his family — the emotional suppression technique that defines his entire character
causalEvents.loreDesc: The formative trauma that creates Mob's coping mechanism — his family's fear of his power forces him to develop emotional suppression as a survival strategy.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A family-rejection event — the discovery that his own family fears him creates the emotional suppression pattern that defines Mob's power management technique.

causalEvents.name: Mob Meets Reigen
causalEvents.trigger: Mob takes a part-time job at Reigen's Spirit Consultation office
causalEvents.consequence: Mob finds a mentor who accepts him without fearing his power — Reigen becomes the emotional intelligence guide Mob's family couldn't be
causalEvents.loreDesc: The mentorship pivot — Reigen's acceptance of Mob despite knowing about his power creates the first relationship where Mob doesn't have to suppress himself completely.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A mentorship introduction event — finding a guide who can provide emotional intelligence that Mob's own family couldn't creates the conditions for Mob's eventual growth.

causalEvents.name: Teruki's Defeat
causalEvents.trigger: Teruki Hanazawa challenges Mob and is defeated without Mob going 100%
causalEvents.consequence: Teruki learns that natural talent is meaningless without genuine human connection — his entire worldview is challenged
causalEvents.loreDesc: The talent questioning — Teruki's defeat forces him to confront that his entire identity was built on power comparisons, not genuine self-worth.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A talent-vs-character revelation — the arrogant natural talent is defeated by the suppressed esper who has genuine human connections. This proves the corruption theorem: Teruki's power was hollow because it was all he had.

causalEvents.name: Mob Chooses Connection Over Power
causalEvents.trigger: Mob is about to go 100% against Koyama; the Body Improvement Club reminds him of his friendship
causalEvents.consequence: Mob chooses not to release — the first time he consciously controls the 100% threshold through emotional choice rather than suppression
causalEvents.loreDesc: The character development pivot — Mob's choice to reject apocalypse in favor of friendship is the moment his emotional development exceeds his emotional suppression.
causalEvents.systemDesc: A consciousness breakthrough — Mob's first voluntary control over the 100% release through genuine emotional choice rather than forced suppression.

anomalies.name: Mob's 100% Release is Involuntary
anomalies.ruleViolated: Power control should be voluntary — espers should be able to choose their output level
anomalies.loreDesc: Mob's 100% emotional release is involuntary — triggered by emotional overload, not choice. Even after character development, he cannot voluntarily access 100%. This makes his most powerful state a system failure rather than a technique.
anomalies.systemDesc: An involuntary release gate — Mob's nuclear option is a malfunction, not a feature. The system has no voluntary access to the highest power state.

anomalies.name: Reigen Has No Powers But Is Effective
anomalies.ruleViolated: Only psychics can deal with supernatural threats
anomalies.loreDesc: Reigen has zero psychic ability but deals with spirits, espers, and supernatural threats effectively through psychological insight, physical courage, and manipulation of emotional states.
anomalies.systemDesc: A non-esper effectiveness anomaly — Reigen's existence proves that human intelligence at its best can match supernatural power in practical terms.

anomalies.name: Body Improvement Matches esper
anomalies.ruleViolated: Physical training cannot match supernatural powers
anomalies.loreDesc: The Body Improvement Club can physically stand up to esper-level threats through sheer physical training and courage. Koyama standing firm against psychic attack is physically impossible in the power system but emotionally true.
anomalies.systemDesc: A human-potential anomaly — ordinary humans achieving esper-level resistance through physical training and psychological resolve.

counterplay.mechanic: Reigen's psychological insight works against espers because emotional vulnerabilities are the source of esper power. Understanding someone's emotional state lets you predict and counter their psychic output.
counterplay.loreDesc: The human intelligence counter — Reigen defeats espers by understanding their emotional triggers rather than countering their psychic output.

counterplay.mechanic: More emotionally developed espers can remain calm under provocation, preventing the emotional pressure build-up that triggers 100% release.
counterplay.loreDesc: The emotional development counter — espers with genuine emotional intelligence are harder to trigger because they can process emotional events without pressure accumulation.

counterplay.mechanic: Physical training builds psychological resilience that partially resists psychic intimidation. The Body Improvement Club's courage creates a shield against esper psychological attacks.
counterplay.loreDesc: The physical resilience counter — ordinary humans can resist esper-level psychological pressure through physical conditioning and community support.

counterplay.mechanic: Espers with high spiritual affinity can perceive and resist spirit possession. Mob's high spiritual affinity lets him see through Ekubo's initial manipulation.
counterplay.loreDesc: The spiritual perception counter — espers can resist spirit manipulation because they can perceive the spiritual entities clearly.

counterplay.mechanic: When Mob is reminded of his genuine friendships, his emotional pressure drops, preventing 100% release. Friendship is the system-level counter to psychic暴走.
counterplay.loreDesc: The connection paradox counter — genuine human connection prevents the emotional pressure accumulation that triggers the 100% release state.

counterplay.mechanic: Mob's emotional suppression technique prevents telepathic reading — Sho's telepathy works by reading emotional states, and if there are no emotions to read, there's nothing to access.
counterplay.loreDesc: The blank-slate defense — Mob's deliberate emotional suppression creates a telepathic blank that cannot be read.
